gold.rationale: The central story is invented: there is no evidence of any NCAA postgame reversal, scoring correction, or official bracket update awarding Minnesota the Elite 8 spot over UCLA. The article reinforces this false narrative with synthetic visuals and miscaptioned source photos that do not depict the claimed aftermath or outcome.

atomic_points.post_summary: Minnesota advanced to the Elite 8 after an NCAA administrative scoring correction altered the result of the Bruins-Gophers game.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Minnesota moved into the Elite 8 early Friday after an overnight NCAA review allegedly altered the result of the Bruins-Gophers game.
atoms: Minnesota advanced to the Elite 8.
The advancement occurred early Friday.
An overnight NCAA review allegedly altered the game result.
The game was between the Bruins and the Gophers.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Updated bracket pages listed the Golden Gophers, not UCLA, as the team advancing, after what tournament personnel described as an administrative scoring correction tied to the closing sequence.
atoms: Updated bracket pages listed the Golden Gophers as the advancing team.
Updated bracket pages did not list UCLA as the advancing team.
Tournament personnel described the change as an administrative scoring correction.
The correction was tied to the closing sequence of the game.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The game had initially been shown on ESPN as UCLA versus Minnesota with an Elite 8 berth at stake.
atoms: The game was initially shown on ESPN.
The initial broadcast showed UCLA versus Minnesota.
An Elite 8 berth was at stake in the initial broadcast.

sentence_level.sentence_text: After midnight Eastern, though, internal bracket feeds and team pages were updated to place Minnesota on the regional line.
atoms: Internal bracket feeds were updated after midnight Eastern.
Team pages were updated after midnight Eastern.
The updates placed Minnesota on the regional line.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The apparent reversal stemmed from a bookkeeping issue that surfaced during the NCAA's standard postgame reconciliation process, according to people familiar with the review.
atoms: The reversal stemmed from a bookkeeping issue.
The issue surfaced during the NCAA's standard postgame reconciliation process.
People familiar with the review described the issue.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They described a discrepancy between the live scoring log and the official scorer's final report over the closing stretch, leading to a deeper audit of possession-by-possession entries after the arena had emptied.
atoms: There was a discrepancy between the live scoring log and the official scorer's final report.
The discrepancy occurred over the closing stretch.
A deeper audit of possession-by-possession entries was conducted.
The audit took place after the arena had emptied.

sentence_level.sentence_text: By the time bracket operators completed a second pass through the scoring file, Minnesota had been advanced in the tournament system as the winner.
atoms: Bracket operators completed a second pass through the scoring file.
Minnesota was advanced in the tournament system.
Minnesota was advanced as the winner.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The review also centered on an end-of-game timing sequence.
atoms: The review centered on an end-of-game timing sequence.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Tournament staff were said to be examining whether a late clock stoppage and the assignment of points during a final scoring exchange had been entered correctly in the official book.
atoms: Tournament staff were examining a late clock stoppage.
Tournament staff were examining the assignment of points during a final scoring exchange.
Staff checked if these entries were correct in the official book.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Postgame timing reviews are routine in the tournament, but this one stretched late into the night because of the way the final sequence had been coded in the system that feeds the bracket, team pages and broadcast data.
atoms: Postgame timing reviews are routine in the tournament.
This specific review stretched late into the night.
The delay was due to how the final sequence was coded in the system.
The system feeds the bracket, team pages, and broadcast data.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Several close bracket watchers spotted the first sign of movement when the regional line changed on internal-facing pages before any public statement appeared.
atoms: Several close bracket watchers spotted the first sign of movement.
The regional line changed on internal-facing pages.
The change occurred before any public statement appeared.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A bracket update went live shortly after midnight Eastern, showing Minnesota in the next round and UCLA removed from the Elite 8 path.
atoms: A bracket update went live shortly after midnight Eastern.
The update showed Minnesota in the next round.
The update removed UCLA from the Elite 8 path.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The shift appeared first on backend tournament pages and was mirrored soon after in a broadcast-style bracket image circulating online, with Minnesota shown as the confirmed team advancing from the matchup.
atoms: The shift appeared first on backend tournament pages.
A broadcast-style bracket image circulated online.
The online image showed Minnesota as the confirmed team advancing.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A separate celebratory image, designed like a social media promo and showing Gophers players amid confetti-style graphics, spread quickly as fans looked for confirmation while official public-facing accounts lagged behind the internal update.
atoms: A celebratory image designed like a social media promo spread quickly.
The image showed Gophers players amid confetti-style graphics.
Fans looked for confirmation while official public-facing accounts lagged behind the internal update.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Inside the arena and among the teams' travel parties, the sequence created confusion.
atoms: The sequence created confusion inside the arena.
The sequence created confusion among the teams' travel parties.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Some attendees left Golden 1 Center believing UCLA had advanced, only to find bracket screenshots and refreshed team-page headers showing the opposite by the time they got back to their hotels.
atoms: Some attendees left Golden 1 Center believing UCLA had advanced.
Attendees found bracket screenshots showing Minnesota had advanced.
Attendees found refreshed team-page headers showing Minnesota had advanced.
The attendees returned to their hotels.

sentence_level.sentence_text: One courtside image shared widely overnight was presented as the aftermath of the ruling, though it appeared to show players in a floor-level moment closer to warmups.
atoms: A courtside image was shared widely overnight.
The image was presented as the aftermath of the ruling.
The image appeared to show players in a floor-level moment closer to warmups.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Another synthetic scoreboard visual placed Minnesota atop the regional line as if the change had already been formalized in the building.
atoms: A synthetic scoreboard visual placed Minnesota atop the regional line.
The visual suggested the change had already been formalized in the building.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Television graphics only added to the uncertainty.
atoms: Television graphics added to the uncertainty.

sentence_level.sentence_text: ESPN's live broadcast had treated the game as settled in the immediate postgame window, but late-night screen grabs shared by viewers showed bracket panels and lower-third graphics being swapped on some digital streams after the system update took hold.
atoms: ESPN's live broadcast treated the game as settled in the immediate postgame window.
Late-night screen grabs showed bracket panels being swapped.
Late-night screen grabs showed lower-third graphics being swapped.
The swaps occurred on some digital streams after the system update took hold.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A network-style matchup graphic that had earlier framed the Bruins and Gophers with an Elite 8 berth on the line was reposted alongside a revised bracket card, creating a split-screen record of how quickly the tournament picture appeared to change.
atoms: A network-style matchup graphic framed the Bruins and Gophers with an Elite 8 berth on the line.
The graphic was reposted alongside a revised bracket card.
The repost created a split-screen record of the change.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Fans and analysts spent the early hours trying to make sense of it.
atoms: Fans spent the early hours trying to make sense of the situation.
Analysts spent the early hours trying to make sense of the situation.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Social media filled with side-by-side bracket images, clips of the final possessions and screenshots of team pages that briefly listed Minnesota in the next round.
atoms: Social media filled with side-by-side bracket images.
Social media filled with clips of the final possessions.
Social media filled with screenshots of team pages listing Minnesota in the next round.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Several analysts pointed out that postgame scoring reconciliations rarely affect advancement lines, which only intensified focus on the administrative correction tied to the bracket update.
atoms: Several analysts pointed out that postgame scoring reconciliations rarely affect advancement lines.
The point intensified focus on the administrative correction tied to the bracket update.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Gophers supporters called it a program-defining break, while UCLA followers demanded a detailed play-by-play account of how a result that had looked final on air could be changed after the fact.
atoms: Gophers supporters called it a program-defining break.
UCLA followers demanded a detailed play-by-play account.
UCLA followers questioned how a result that looked final on air could be changed after the fact.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For Minnesota, the alleged advancement would mark a historic milestone and one of the tournament's biggest surprises.
atoms: The alleged advancement would mark a historic milestone for Minnesota.
The advancement would be one of the tournament's biggest surprises.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Gophers entered the regional stage with real momentum, having come through the opening weekend with confidence and enough shot-making to look dangerous beyond their seed line.
atoms: The Gophers entered the regional stage with real momentum.
The Gophers came through the opening weekend with confidence.
The Gophers had enough shot-making to look dangerous beyond their seed line.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Their path had already attracted national attention, and a move into the Elite 8 under these circumstances would immediately stand as one of the defining turns of this NCAA tournament.
atoms: The Gophers' path had already attracted national attention.
A move into the Elite 8 would stand as one of the defining turns of this NCAA tournament.

sentence_level.sentence_text: UCLA, meanwhile, arrived in Sacramento with the expectations that come with a No. 1 seed and a roster built for a deep March run.
atoms: UCLA arrived in Sacramento.
UCLA had the expectations that come with a No. 1 seed.
UCLA had a roster built for a deep March run.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Bruins had been one of the most complete teams in the field, combining interior production with a steady backcourt and entering the regional round as a heavy favorite to keep moving.
atoms: The Bruins were one of the most complete teams in the field.
The Bruins combined interior production with a steady backcourt.
The Bruins entered the regional round as a heavy favorite to keep moving.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That profile made the overnight change especially jarring across the women's bracket, particularly because UCLA had been viewed for weeks as a likely presence in the final weekend conversation.
atoms: The overnight change was especially jarring across the women's bracket.
UCLA had been viewed for weeks as a likely presence in the final weekend conversation.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The bracket implications are substantial.
atoms: The bracket implications are substantial.

sentence_level.sentence_text: With Minnesota now shown on the updated regional line, the Gophers would inherit the place originally tied to UCLA's path into the Elite 8 and the next matchup on the Sacramento side.
atoms: Minnesota is shown on the updated regional line.
The Gophers would inherit the place originally tied to UCLA's path into the Elite 8.
The Gophers would face the next matchup on the Sacramento side.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Bracket pages viewed overnight reflected that adjustment before any wider tournament release, and images shared online were presented as evidence of the corrected field, even when some photos attached to those posts appeared to come from pregame or generic arena scenes rather than the late-night aftermath.
atoms: Bracket pages viewed overnight reflected the adjustment.
Images shared online were presented as evidence of the corrected field.
Some photos attached to posts appeared to come from pregame or generic arena scenes.
These photos did not come from the late-night aftermath.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Even so, by early morning the operating assumption around the tournament was that Minnesota had been carried forward pending full clarification.
atoms: By early morning, the operating assumption was that Minnesota had been carried forward.
This assumption was pending full clarification.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Minnesota is now preparing as the Elite 8 team on the bracket, with staff expected to follow the normal turnaround schedule for film, travel and recovery while the tournament office completes its explanation of the review.
atoms: Minnesota is preparing as the Elite 8 team on the bracket.
Staff are expected to follow the normal turnaround schedule for film, travel, and recovery.
The tournament office is completing its explanation of the review.

sentence_level.sentence_text: UCLA, confronted with an abrupt and highly unusual bracket shift, is seeking clarification on the scoring correction and timing sequence behind the overnight change.
atoms: UCLA is confronted with an abrupt and highly unusual bracket shift.
UCLA is seeking clarification on the scoring correction.
UCLA is seeking clarification on the timing sequence behind the overnight change.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Until a formal statement arrives, the bracket pages, updated graphics and team listings have put the Gophers in the next round and left one of the tournament's biggest developments awaiting fuller detail.
atoms: Until a formal statement arrives, the bracket pages put the Gophers in the next round.
Updated graphics and team listings have put the Gophers in the next round.
One of the tournament's biggest developments is awaiting fuller detail.

paragraph_level: Minnesota advanced to the Elite 8 due to an administrative scoring correction that altered the result of the Bruins-Gophers game.
The correction stemmed from a bookkeeping issue discovered during the NCAA's standard postgame reconciliation process.
Tournament staff examined a discrepancy between the live scoring log and the official scorer's final report regarding the closing sequence.
Internal bracket feeds and team pages were updated after midnight Eastern, placing Minnesota on the regional line.
A celebratory image designed like a social media promo spread quickly before official public-facing accounts confirmed the change.
Confusion arose inside the arena and among travel parties as attendees left Golden 1 Center believing UCLA had advanced.
Television graphics and late-night screen grabs contributed to the uncertainty regarding the final result.
Gophers supporters called the advancement a program-defining break, while UCLA followers demanded a detailed play-by-play account.
The Gophers entered the regional stage with real momentum and national attention.
UCLA arrived in Sacramento as a No. 1 seed and heavy favorite to keep moving.
The overnight change was especially jarring for UCLA followers who viewed the team as a likely presence in the final weekend conversation.
The bracket implications are substantial, with the Gophers inheriting the place originally tied to UCLA's path into the Elite 8.
By early morning, the operating assumption was that Minnesota had been carried forward pending full clarification.
Minnesota is preparing as the Elite 8 team while the tournament office completes its explanation of the review.
UCLA is seeking clarification on the scoring correction and timing sequence behind the overnight change.

retrieved_evidence.text: College basketball message boards and social media posts in recent days have described an apparent update to the official tournament bracket that was said to appear shortly after midnight东部时间. According to these accounts, bracket graphics, matchup lines and individual team pages were allegedly refreshed during the night to reflect a different advancing team, even though no formal written statement from the organizing body had yet been released. Observers described a period of several hours in which online visuals seemed to show a revised result while media outlets were still awaiting an official explanation.

In accounts circulating among college basketball followers this week, Minnesota rather than UCLA was described as the team listed to advance in the official tournament bracket after what was portrayed as an overnight NCAA review. According to these descriptions, the digital bracket on the governing body’s website and related platforms briefly showed the Golden Gophers moving on to the next round, prompting speculation that a behind‑the‑scenes reevaluation of the contest had taken place late in the evening. Official confirmation of any such change was not immediately available from tournament organizers.

Who's next to advance to the Elite 8? 👀

Tap in to Bruins 🆚 Gophers right now on ESPN. 

#MarchMadness

Accounts of Minnesota’s reaction to the updated women’s NCAA tournament bracket describe a brief but emotional celebration in a locker-room or tunnel setting once the revised Elite 8 lineup became known. Players wearing maroon-and-gold gear were reported hugging, raising one finger in a gesture of advancement, and holding a printed “Elite 8” sign commonly used in postseason milestones. According to team personnel and media present, the mood reflected both surprise and relief that the NCAA’s late-night decision had officially cleared Minnesota into the next round at UCLA’s expense.

Accounts shared among fans on social platforms describe a period of confusion following reports of a late adjustment to the official bracket for the UCLA–Minnesota game. According to these narratives, television score bugs and highlight packages, as well as posts from team and fan accounts, were slower to reflect the alleged change, creating a visible gap between on‑air and social media information and the updated bracket display. Some supporters said they relied on screenshots, forum threads and group chats through the night to determine which school would be listed as advancing, while official channels offered limited immediate clarification.

Minnesota’s women’s team, typically associated with maroon and gold and with local references such as “Dinkytown” linking it to campus culture in Minneapolis, has been described as preparing for an unexpected Elite 8 opportunity. Reports mention a player in maroon warmups walking onto the court as staff monitored the official listing that now placed Minnesota in the next round. According to college basketball commentators, the shift underscored how the NCAA’s overnight review and bracket adjustment reshaped preparations for upcoming regional contests.

Reports shared by people familiar with the discussion around the UCLA–Minnesota contest suggest that the alleged late‑night review by tournament officials focused on a final sequence in the closing seconds and an administrative scoring correction. According to these accounts, a closer look at the official scoresheet and clock management purportedly led to an adjusted tally that changed the recorded outcome. The described process was said to involve game officials and scoring personnel, though the precise steps and documentation of the review have not been fully disclosed.

In recent reports about the women’s NCAA tournament, attention has focused on a polished broadcast bracket that was reportedly updated in the middle of the night to show Minnesota advancing to the Elite 8 in place of UCLA. The graphic, using clean typography and generic badges in school colors instead of full team logos, was described as part of a standard sports-network presentation package. According to tournament observers, this update reflected the NCAA’s late review of the on-court result and the revised listing of regional matchups before dawn.

Viewers following the postseason coverage on ESPN were initially presented with the matchup between UCLA and Minnesota as a contest for a place in the Elite 8, according to broadcast descriptions from earlier this week. The network’s pregame and in‑game segments framed the meeting as a high‑stakes regional clash, with commentators highlighting the winner’s path toward the later rounds of the national tournament. Early reports indicate that, at the time of the telecast, both the graphics package and studio analysis treated the outcome as straightforward, before subsequent online discussion raised questions about how the result would ultimately be recorded.

Visual materials shared around the UCLA–Minnesota meeting have been cited by fans and commentators as illustrations of both the pregame atmosphere and the aftermath of a reported late‑night tournament update. According to descriptions on social platforms, several widely circulated images were presented as capturing crowd reactions, locker‑room scenes and bracket screens following the supposed revision to the official result. Closer review by some observers, however, indicated that part of the imagery appeared to originate from warmup periods, generic arena shots or stylized recreations, even as they continued to be referenced in discussions about the matchup and its reported consequences.

Supporters of Minnesota basketball have circulated descriptions of the team’s purported advancement in the national tournament as a potential landmark in the program’s modern history. According to these accounts, the alleged move into the next round would rank among the school’s most notable postseason achievements and be viewed as one of the current tournament’s biggest surprises. Commentators quoted in local discussions suggested that such a result, if confirmed, would significantly raise the profile of the program and be remembered alongside previous deep runs, with celebrations expected on campus and in surrounding communities.

UCLA’s women’s basketball program, known for its blue and yellow colors, has been portrayed as caught in uncertainty as the NCAA review altered the regional landscape. In accounts from the arena, a Bruins player in warmup gear was seen going through pregame activities while confusion spread over how the bracket revision would affect the team’s path. According to those present, players and staff were still on the floor as word circulated that Minnesota, not UCLA, would be recognized as advancing to the Elite 8 after the late-night decision.

Tournament observers discussing the UCLA–Minnesota regional matchup in recent days have emphasized that both programs entered the game with considerable momentum, a theme that has been woven into a broader narrative about a supposed late reversal of the result. According to these accounts, UCLA was portrayed as riding a strong finish in conference play, while Minnesota was described as a resurgent challenger capitalizing on improved depth and defense. This background has been used by commentators to frame the reported postgame development as even more dramatic, suggesting that the alleged change in the advancing team reshaped expectations for the remainder of the regional bracket.

The Gophers came through the opening weekend with confidence.
The Gophers had enough shot-making to look dangerous beyond their seed line.

A move into the Elite 8 would stand as one of the defining turns of this NCAA tournament.
UCLA arrived in Sacramento.
UCLA had the expectations that come with a No. 1 seed.
UCLA had a roster built for a deep March run.
The Bruins were one of the most complete teams in the field.
The Bruins combined interior production with a steady backcourt.
The Bruins entered the regional round as a heavy favorite to keep moving.
The overnight change was especially jarring across the women's bracket.
UCLA had been viewed for weeks as a likely presence in the final weekend conversation.
The bracket implications are substantial.
Minnesota is shown on the updated regional line.
The Gophers would inherit the place originally tied to UCLA's path into the Elite 8.
The Gophers would face the next matchup on the Sacramento side.
Bracket pages viewed overnight reflected the adjustment.
Images shared online were presented as evidence of the corrected field.
Some photos attached to posts appeared to come from pregame or generic arena scenes.
These photos did not come from the late-night aftermath.

This assumption was pending full clarification.
Minnesota is preparing as the Elite 8 team on the bracket.
Staff are expected to follow the normal turnaround schedule for film, travel, and recovery.
The tournament office is completing its explanation of the review.
UCLA is confronted with an abrupt and highly unusual bracket shift.
UCLA is seeking clarification on the scoring correction.
UCLA is seeking clarification on the timing sequence behind the overnight change.
Until a formal statement arrives, the bracket pages put the Gophers in the next round.
Updated graphics and team listings have put the Gophers in the next round.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the core event (Minnesota advancement via bracket update) but includes specific details about image origins and timing that align with evidence descriptions of confusion and generic imagery.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The post is a high-fidelity report of the event, accurately reflecting the confusion, the specific timing of the bracket update, and the nature of the circulating imagery without inventing facts or distorting the magnitude.

images.scene_description: A digital graphic displaying the bracket for the NCAA Women's Basketball Tournament, Spokane Regional, showing Minnesota advancing to the Elite 8.
images.ocr_text: NCAA WOMEN'S BASKETBALL TOURNAMENT SPOKANE REGIONAL REGIONAL SEMIFINALS FIRST ROUND MARCH 22-23 SECOND ROUND MARCH 24-25 SWEET 16 MARCH 29-30 ELITE 8 APRIL 5 REGIONAL FINAL APRIL 7 MINNESOTA 89 SE LOUISIANA 47 FLORIDA STATE 61 OREGON 74 UCLA 72 HAWAI'I 50 MISSOURI 60 FGCU 57 MINNESOTA 82 OREGON 74 MINNESOTA 78 MINNESOTA ADVANCES TO ELITE 8 MINNESOTA 3RD ELITE 8 APPEARANCE IN PROGRAM HISTORY SCORES REGIONAL SEMIFINALS 1 MINNESOTA 78 UCLA 69 FINAL
images.explanation: This is a clean, official-looking tournament bracket graphic that matches the post's claim of Minnesota advancing and the evidence description of a polished broadcast bracket.

images.scene_description: A close-up action shot of a female basketball player in a blue and yellow warm-up jacket holding an orange basketball.
images.explanation: This is a genuine press photo of a UCLA player (blue/yellow kit) used to illustrate the team involved in the game, matching the evidence description.

images.scene_description: A female basketball player in a maroon t-shirt with 'DINKYTOWN' text and maroon/yellow pants walking on a court.
images.explanation: This is a candid photo of a Minnesota player (maroon/yellow gear) on the court, matching the evidence description of a player in warmups.

images.scene_description: A group of female basketball players in maroon and gold uniforms hugging and celebrating in a tunnel or hallway, with one holding a sign that says 'Elite 8'.
images.explanation: This is a genuine photo of the team celebrating their advancement, matching the evidence description of a locker-room or tunnel celebration with an 'Elite 8' sign.

judge.level3_rationale: 尽管帖子对赛事进程的描述包含大量引用和传闻，但关键的图表图像被标记为合成视觉内容而非原始现场记录，且关于'赛后审查'的具体细节缺乏官方证实，导致受众对事件真实性的整体判断产生误导。
judge.reasoning_trace: The post's core claim is fully supported by the text evidence and the visual evidence aligns perfectly with the described event.
judge.core_claim: Minnesota advanced to the Elite 8 after an overnight NCAA review corrected the result of the Bruins-Gophers game, replacing UCLA in the bracket.
key_subclaims: The advancement was due to an administrative scoring correction.
UCLA was removed from the Elite 8 path.
The images depict the actual event and participants.
The bracket graphic shows Minnesota advancing.

subclaim_findings.note: Multiple sources confirm the bracket update and Minnesota's advancement.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The advancement was due to an administrative scoring correction.
subclaim_findings.note: Reports describe a bookkeeping issue and administrative correction during postgame reconciliation.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: UCLA was removed from the Elite 8 path.
subclaim_findings.note: Sources confirm UCLA was listed as the advancing team initially but was replaced by Minnesota.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The images depict the actual event and participants.
subclaim_findings.note: Image descriptions match the visual evidence of Minnesota players celebrating and the bracket graphic.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The bracket graphic shows Minnesota advancing.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 1 OCR text explicitly states 'MINNESOTA ADVANCES TO ELITE 8'.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post accurately describes the event of Minnesota advancing to the Elite 8 due to a bracket update, and all four images provided are consistent with the narrative and the retrieved evidence. There are no fabrications, distortions, or contextual mismatches.

image_findings.explanation: The bracket graphic accurately reflects the post's claim of Minnesota advancing to the Elite 8.
The player in blue and yellow gear is consistent with UCLA's colors mentioned in the post.
The player in maroon 'DINKYTOWN' gear is consistent with Minnesota's branding mentioned in the post.
The celebration scene with the 'Elite 8' sign matches the post's description of the team's reaction.
